scenario_title: Delete absent RR with satisfied prerequisite
test_rationale: This scenario isolates the underspecified corner case by first ensuring the target name and A RRset exist, so the prerequisite passes cleanly, and then issuing a delete_rr for an A record that is not present in that RRset. The post-state is expected to remain unchanged either way, so the only reliable oracle is the UPDATE reply code, which is where BIND and Knot may differ on whether absent-member deletion is a no-op or an error.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rr of an absent RR as a no-op and accepts the UPDATE.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE when the target RR is not present, despite the prerequisite being satisfied.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.2 and §3.4.2 define prerequisite evaluation and the delete_rr syntax, but do not explicitly specify the outcome when an UPDATE requests deletion of a single RR that is absent while the relevant RRset exists. That leaves room for implementations to either succeed idempotently or fail the transaction, producing wire-visible RCODE divergence.
